### kathylu — 2012-08-02T02:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-cream-making-using-ordering-helpful-hints-in-this-thread-pls.2777/post-42391>

> **Cavemam;42605 wrote:** ok, so do we think for topical we can skip the dissolving it? This is gonna be a crowed sourced product!
>
>
>
> And, what should the ratio of dhea to cream be?

This is just a suggestion, but I'd make a 1% cream. So the ratio would be 1 gram dhea in 100 ml of carrier base. Then each ml would contain 10 mg of dhea. I would start out using no more than 2 ml per day (20 mg) unless you plan to monitor blood levels (better to be safe!). I think you may have some issues with the dhea separating out if you just use coconut oil. I think you will need an emulsifier, such as 1 gram of cosmetic grade plant wax (not beeswax or paraffin!), although trying 95 % ethanol might be worthwhile.

Also, I wonder if using it directly on the face is good or not...dhea can cause hair growth. Just sayin.

Right now my evil scientists plan (pulling out the breville super whipper) is to make a solution of DHEA/Alcohol and try to make an emulsion that is a 2% DHEA/98% other stuff that I toss in. With CO, Vanilla, Shea Butter and Vit E capsule if I have some or get some while out.

I still feel like I'm flying blind and a wastin time - I was hoping more people would chime in. . .or you know, you go play for the other team and start dating a cute lady compounding pharmacist! Think how helpful that would be for all of us!

### chocolate — 2012-08-05T18:24:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-cream-making-using-ordering-helpful-hints-in-this-thread-pls.2777/post-43074>

[http://zrtdocsblog.blogspot.com/2010/05/dhea-as-vaginal-treatment.html](http://http://zrtdocsblog.blogspot.com/2010/05/dhea-as-vaginal-treatment.html)

dhea as a vaginal treatment

> Until then, what can you do if you want to try it out? DHEA is an OTC supplement and any compounding pharmacy should be able to create a vaginal suppository or vaginal cream similar to those used in the studies. The studies have used dosages of 3.25mg to 13mg. Since the 3.25 mg dosage worked as well as the 13 mg I would not see any reason for using the higher dosage. In fact, since you are going for a localized effect, my educated guess is that even Â½ that dosage – 1.5 would work well for many women. Using DHEA may also have a higher safety rating than using vaginal testosterone since you are unlikely to trigger irreversible clitoral hypertrophy which maybe caused rarely by testosterone especially at higher dosages.

### Glamazon — 2012-08-12T00:20:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-cream-making-using-ordering-helpful-hints-in-this-thread-pls.2777/post-44411>

I think I am going to try my own compounding. Going to the science supply store tomorrow. It is filled with wonderful things like beakers and glass stirring rods and cobalt blue bottles. They cater to the artisanal beauty crowd who do their own creams and aromatherapy.

Found this today: DHEA will dissolve in fats, oils and alcohol. Liquid DHEA, which is usually DHEA dissolved in alcohol, is now available and is is claimed to be up to 4-5 times as well absorbed as DHEA capsules. (My own personal experiments indicate that DHEA dissolved in alcohol is twice as well absorbed as capsules.) DHEA capsules are somewhat better absorbed if taken with food.

[http://www.futurescience.com/dhea.html](http://http://www.futurescience.com/dhea.html)

### Glamazon — 2012-08-12T02:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-cream-making-using-ordering-helpful-hints-in-this-thread-pls.2777/post-44424>

I also found this simple compounding formula on a pheromone forum:

To dissolve the DHEA, I emptied the capsule into a dropper bottle, added the oil, shook it very well, sat the bottle in a hot water bath, and continued to shake periodically. For best results I'd say give it a day or two of sitting around, and just shake it for a few seconds whenever you walk by, and re-heat your hot water bath whenever you get a chance.

There are extra fillers in the capsule that you will have to filter out after you have given the DHEA time to dissolve. I used a coffee filter, and it worked okay but not great. I'm hoping to think of a better solution for filtering the schmutz, so let me know if you think of one.

Also, if you use coconut oil make sure its fractionated coconut oil, not the whole coconut oil you can buy at the grocery store. That stuff is good to eat but will be greasy on the skin and probably wouldn't dissolve in alcohol very well. You could also dissolve the DHEA into DPG, jojoba, or perfumer's alcohol (or everclear if it's legal where you live. I can't get it in Chicago.)

[http://pherotruth.com/Thread-DHEA--4574](http://http://pherotruth.com/Thread-DHEA--4574)

### Glamazon — 2012-08-12T18:31:37+0300

> **Cavemam;44755 wrote:** What's this store? That sounds like fun. Want to go out for coffee when I come down!

I was going to go today but I spaced that it is closed on Sunday. [http://www.sciencecompany.com/](http://http://www.sciencecompany.com/)

It is on 1st and Lincoln. Lincoln is one way at that point, going North. Best would be to come down I 25 and get off at the Alameda exit (so much construction there so be careful) and go North on Lincoln. It is on the west side of Lincoln and parking is in the back. It's a little building on the corner of a parking lot. It's very close to downtown. Avoid rush hour at all costs. Don't want to spike that Cortisol.

I will PM you my cell today. Just make sure your in box has room! Let me know when to PM.

If you kid is a sciency kind of guy there's a lot stuff for kids to buy like weather stations and chemistry kits.

You could outfit a right good lab if you so desired.

### Glamazon — 2012-08-19T07:03:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-cream-making-using-ordering-helpful-hints-in-this-thread-pls.2777/post-45662>

I used one gram of no additive DHEA mixed with 100 mls of warm melted Coconut oil. The DHEA didnt go into total solution. Some particulate remained. But, I put the beaker in ice bath still it started to get thick like maple syrup. I kept stirring it while it sat in the ice bath. Then I poured it into a glass covered container and put it in the freezer to finish the thickening process. This way I hoped to keep all the particles suspended as evenly as I can. When I put in on my skin there was no grainy feeling.

# Thread: Seniors Optimized living in aged care
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seniors-optimized-living-in-aged-care.2796/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jimmy82 — 2012-08-01T10:04:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seniors-optimized-living-in-aged-care.2796/post-42229>

Many oldies or the senior citizens who don't get proper care and proper attention at home they used to move to an independent living centers or the aged care centers but i must say that many of the seniors care centers do offer very versatile life over there but when some one reaches there the real picture is not exactly like that and i came to know for a fact when i visited my uncle in New York..

No doubt they were providing their very best services but some of the features that were mentioned on their brochure was not really available .....

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-01T23:05:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seniors-optimized-living-in-aged-care.2796/post-42340>

this is a huge issue for elders.......you eat their food you meet your maker faster.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-02T00:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seniors-optimized-living-in-aged-care.2796/post-42357>

I heard of one guy doing fairly well in a nursing home. He ate mostly eggs, lots of eggs. I guess they gave him some latitude on what he ate. They probably don't have seafood available very often. People do seem to decline quickly in such places.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-02T05:48:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seniors-optimized-living-in-aged-care.2796/post-42441>

One of the reasons I went back to Michigan was to investigate housing etc for tribal seniors. yikes what an eye opener! One place had some tribal housing but it was way out in the country and even with their sporadically provided transportation it was bad and the waiting list is 10 years! I don't know if someone was trying to pull my leg but I was told some elders where pretty far down the highway to town pushing their walkers before they got picked up.

The other place was a converted motel for senior housing and in very bad condition.

I went to a couple of "elders lunches." One would think with all the excellent wild white fish available it would be a main staple at these meals. It was all white bread (lots) commercial deli type cole slaw, fake butter and horrors, commodity cheese! (I guess you have to be Indian to fully appreciate that last phrase.) Disgusting! I took a cup of tea and told them I got up late and just ate breakfast. Of course, white frosted cake for desert. The other place I went to was a church pot luck. Typical crap food passed off as "They always provide a nice spread." So much for the so called influence of the tribal diabetes program. By this time of the trip I had even given up drinking any tap water and stuck to a type of purified water I found. We used a combo of an electric hot plate or gas stove and had wild white fish or buffalo steaks with all the trimmings and of course plenty of bacon at the many beautiful parks in the area. I suspected it would be like this but I had to see for myself, still it was very disappointing.

### Pamela — 2012-08-13T17:20:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seniors-optimized-living-in-aged-care.2796/post-44686>

My father is in a nursing home and what they eat is horrible. Seeing these people and how they "live" in these places really gets me working toward optimal for myself. It is not an option to go in one of those places.

### Cyryl — 2013-07-02T21:52:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seniors-optimized-living-in-aged-care.2796/post-89401>

Private well known old age care homes provide the best facilities to old people. There is a nursing staff available 24/7 for the care of old people. So check the good old age care facility for your parents.

### fitness@home — 2013-07-03T00:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seniors-optimized-living-in-aged-care.2796/post-89405>

Years ago, I was employed at the nicest nursing home in Monroe County, MI. Worked in their office and often typed up the menus for the head of the Dietary Dept.

I have to say that it was _very_ heavy on carbs at breakfast (cereals, oatmeal, toast, juice). They did a reasonable job of getting protein and veggies in at lunch and dinner, but there was always a carb side dish, bread, and dessert included. They pretty much followed the USDA food pyramid recommendations. Sad...very sad.

Although it is a very nice nursing care facility, I hope to never need such a place.

### caroline — 2013-07-03T05:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seniors-optimized-living-in-aged-care.2796/post-89421>

We really need to work very hard not to wind up in a nursing home - trouble is we may not have the option.........

My really dear friend and I are giving her sons explicit instructions on how to care for us if need be....

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-02T05:07:37+0300

Whatever you hold in your mind will tend to occur in your life. If you continue to believe as you have always believed, you will continue to act as you have always acted. Make your choices and you create your life.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-02T20:56:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ode-to-heavy-whipping-cream.2800/post-42542>

> **Cavemam;42797 wrote:** Well, I can't tell if Dr. K was poo-pooing or approving this thread - but it's helping me! I like hearing your stories and that other people are giving up this or that - and that most of you are approaching it from a logical standpoint and not a woo woo.
>
>
>
> I'm doing Kerrygold in coffee today.

I understood his comment as neither. What I got out of it was that if you are not getting what you would like to have, then you need to change your mind first in the process of achieving new results. You're unlikely to achieve something by hanging on to the "old" thing in your mind.... the great thick cream and how you're gonna miss it and if it's \*really\* necessary to eliminate it. Start thinking in terms of the results you're going for..... and the cream will go with not regret b/c you're focused on allowing something new, better, more like what you were looking for. And then it will happen. The cream might never be an issue again whether or not you'll eventually be able to eat it. B/c you're now where you wanted to be.

As for whether or not you should have cream, no matter how thick or buttery, my honest opinion is that things aren't working the way you would like them to, there's quite a few things that suggest that you have an ED-type problem going on, EDs need to follow AI protocol. There is no cream, there is no butter. But that is your choice to make. You can stick with what hasn't been working, or you can move on to new grounds. The new grounds might have nothing to do with cream or no cream.

As usual, YMMV

### Inger — 2012-08-03T10:10:25+0300

### Mackay — 2012-08-03T13:23:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ode-to-heavy-whipping-cream.2800/post-42651>

For what it's worth, there is the whole discussion about A1 milk versus A2 milk.

A1 milk has a protein configuration that gives it a similar quality to wheat is messing up your health.

A2 milk does not have this problem and some folks report that they are fine on A2 milk. Products like ghee have almost all of the proteins taken out, so there should be few problems with it.

Butter still has some protein in it, so some folks will have a problem if it is made from A1 milk.

By the way, all goats milk is A2.

### Michael — 2012-08-03T16:30:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ode-to-heavy-whipping-cream.2800/post-42667>

> **Mackay;42930 wrote:** For what it's worth, there is the whole discussion about A1 milk versus A2 milk.

Now I've heard people in paleo (some big names) say that A1/A2 is all a bit airy and there's nothing proved. Makes me wonder if they ever looked into it much. But if you hear Woodforde speak and give him a fair hearing .... I don't think he ever said he had definite proof. But what he has got is a plausible mechanism, some good rodent experiments, some suggestive stuff the Russians have done with autistic kids, and a ton of personal testimony. Any of those might not mean much on its own, but if you put them all together, it really does seem to add up to something.

### kathylu — 2012-08-03T16:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ode-to-heavy-whipping-cream.2800/post-42675>

The tack that I and my family are taking is to avoid dairy as much as possible. When we do indulge, I try to make it raw A2 dairy. I am finding I don't really miss it that much and interestingly neither does my 5 year old. He prefers to drink Kevita instead of milk.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42321>

**Report successes, failures, questions, and answers (dead-end or not ) here! Everything goes**

I've been pretty hardcore at it  Inger's Cocktails, raw seafood as ceviche, pickled or salted, fish roe by the tub full, shrimp with head, antenna, shell and all, piles o'seaweed,.... I'd say I'm seeing some progress. Mood is fun-tastic today, no AN thoughts or other ED behaviors, energy is returning.

I ran out of fish roe today. How important is it to my success? Do I need to get out into my car for another 2 hour roundtrip on Denver's crazy highways STAT? Or can I let it go for a little while.....

### shilohman — 2012-08-01T23:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42351>

Question, what is everyone doing to keep their omega 6/3 ratio from getting out of whack i.e. too much omega 3 vs omega 6? I seem to be having a problem with this. Been feeling tired and run down plus been getting what I call omega 3 headaches. I know its not my hormones, nothing hormone wise has changed since I started with the 1/2 lb of fish a day. Have upped my consumption of Brazil nuts from 1 a day to 5 a day which seems to help some. How are others coping with this or you not seeing this type of problem?

### nonchalant — 2012-08-02T00:00:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42353>

Hi Shilohman, I had cravings for nuts after a few weeks of seafood every day. I went ahead and indulged, and that craving has gone away. Don't know if it's related to the omega 6/3 ratio, because I haven't tested yet. But that will come soon...

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-02T00:11:21+0300

Wondering what to do with the leftover masago from the sushi I made last night. I love the taste of it and thought of putting it on top of eggs for my bf (no eggs for me).

I also have made some shrimp broth from the heads and exoskeletons. Can I just grind up the heads too in this broth mixture or should I save it as a pure broth? I remember Dr. K saying he blends it all up but I wasn't sure about the heads. Considering there are eyes on these shrimps I thought it would be a good idea. Thoughts?

### AKMan — 2012-08-02T01:29:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42374>

> **shilohman;42606 wrote: Question, what is everyone doing to keep their omega 6/3 ratio from getting out of whack i.e. too much omega 3 vs omega 6? I seem to be having a problem with this. Been feeling tired and run down plus been getting what I call omega 3 headaches. I know its not my hormones, nothing hormone wise has changed since I started with the 1/2 lb of fish a day. Have upped my consumption of Brazil nuts from 1 a day to 5 a day which seems to help some. How are others coping with this or you not seeing this type of problem?
>
>
> From what I've read, if you aren't supplementing O3 (fish or krill oil) then your ratios will be fine without any intervention. I wouldn't willingly eat something high in PUFA just to get my O6 up. It can take years to clear the O6s from your cells, so having just started this epi-paleo, I doubt your ratios are less than ideal. If in doubt, get tested after eating epi-paleo for 6 months or so. I would be willing to bet your ratios would be around 5 or 6 to 1.
>
>
>
> Look at this chart showing O3/O6 in commons foods, even seafood has O6. You are probably getting more than you realize.[http://paleozonenutrition.files.wordpress.com/2011/05/omega-3-and-6-in-fats-oils-nuts-seeds-meat-and-seafood-2.pdf](http://http://paleozonenutrition.files.wordpress.com/2011/05/omega-3-and-6-in-fats-oils-nuts-seeds-meat-and-seafood-2.pdf)
>
>
>
> Patty Cakes;42614 wrote:** I also have made some shrimp broth from the heads and exoskeletons. Can I just grind up the heads too in this broth mixture or should I save it as a pure broth? I remember Dr. K saying he blends it all up but I wasn't sure about the heads. Considering there are eyes on these shrimps I thought it would be a good idea. Thoughts?

Put it all in! I feel so bad about all the good food I've thrown away over the years. I thought the only way to eat fish was rolled in flour, fried in vegetable oil, and eaten alongside hushpuppies. I have been making an awesome fish broth this summer with salmon livers, hearts, heads, and bones. The skin makes it taste too fishy, but the bones and organs taste wonderful.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-02T01:32:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42375>

> Put it all in! I feel so bad about all the good food I've thrown away over the years. I thought the only way to eat fish was rolled in flour, fried in vegetable oil, and eaten alongside hushpuppies. I have been making an awesome fish broth this summer with salmon livers, hearts, heads, and bones. The skin makes it taste too fishy, but the bones and organs taste wonderful.

WAPF says to refrain from using oily fish for fish broth b/c of the long cooking time that would potentially damage the DHA. Heads have lots of DHA. Just FYI.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-02T01:34:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42376>

Fry up that salmon skin! That is a wonderful compliment to a salmon dinner. I just use a spatula to remove the salmon from the skin while still in the pan. While we are eating the salmon the skin continues to cook in a bit of ghee. I flip it a couple of times to keep it from getting too crisp but basically it comes out like a salmon chip! It tops of the meal wonderfully.

I was thinking of making something by grinding it all up that I could just slug down every day for a quick lunch or dinner. I could add herbs and some other good fish to it if I wanted more of a meal. Just trying to get those DHAs in me!

Yep, I'm in with both boots on. And I'm totally addicted to raw roe, too. I've been eating at least 6 T per day. I feel amazing when I eat it - even better than I feel with Inger's Cocktails (which are now whole-fish smoothies, not just the heads).

I, too, had digestive changes (not fun) at first, but they seem to have settled down, at least for now.

And, yes, Patty Cakes you CAN do the shrimp heads. It's mostly psychological. My only advice is don't get shrimp with heads bigger than you can eat in one bite. The other day I ate a batch of wild caught shrimp with heads so big it took two bites to eat them, which was not so nice. I just close my eyes, thank the little shrimp for its goodness, decapitate and chew! (I am not yet doing this raw, though. Soul?)

### chocolate — 2012-08-03T03:16:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42605>

> Fermented Foods
>
>
>
> GABA in food often is synthesized by using lactobacillus and monascus, so several fermented foods contain it. For example, yogurt is one good source of GABA, and some cheeses are another. Korean kimchi also contains high amounts of it. These strains of friendly bacteria and mold, lactobacillus and monascus, are good for use as starters for foods, including cheese, yogurt and other fermented foods, according to "Bioactive Proteins and Peptides as Functional Foods and Nutraceuticals," by Yoshinori Mine.

Read more: [http://www.livestrong.com/article/478780-a-list-of-foods-with-the-highest-gaba/#ixzz22RBamdYo](http://http://www.livestrong.com/article/478780-a-list-of-foods-with-the-highest-gaba/#ixzz22RBamdYo)

I'm gonna look for the kombucha, now that I committed matricide.

If anyone knew of some respectable little tins of caviar, that would be good. I'm just finding the deli sized frozen cartons.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-03T04:02:23+0300

> **Cavemam;42870 wrote:** ok, I need clear direction - will you snap a pic at what you are buying and what section of the market (even if it's asian?). . .my forays into the asian markets here did not make me hunger for seafood for sure. I spend so much time "sourcing" the best quality foods, it just unnerved me a little. I know JK says bad seafood is better than the best beef - but I'm not so sure about that! We have a small Asian area here in San Diego. There are a handful of stores that sell fresh fish....some good some not so good. There is one in particular that I like and buy most of the fresh sushi fish there. I venture into the others but unless they have something special I won't buy fish from them. I found some beautiful fishies today but they were from Japan and the radiation thing will keep me from buying Japanese for quite a while, if at all. I'm not sure there is any "safe" fish with radiation being measured along the pacific coast now for about a year. And China is scary too! But then again so is Korea (I think South) for the problems they had with the Crown Prince Oysters.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-03T05:26:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42626>

> **Cavemam;42870 wrote:** ok, I need clear direction - will you snap a pic at what you are buying and what section of the market (even if it's asian?). . .my forays into the asian markets here did not make me hunger for seafood for sure. I spend so much time "sourcing" the best quality foods, it just unnerved me a little. I know JK says bad seafood is better than the best beef - but I'm not so sure about that! I don't trust China and Japan - they are happy putting all kinds of crazy s\*\*\* into food they sell to USA.

Another option to go with what others have said... get to know your nearest fishmonger. There will be a spawning season for fish in the USA (perhaps timing will be different depending on where in the country you are). At that time, whole fish that are caught will be full of roe in sacs. Most of this is thrown away because it's not salmon/sturgeon. But whenever a fish is gutted and cleaned, there will be roe in many of them during this season. When this time of year arrives, simply ask for all the roe that they find. I'm sure it will be cheap. It will be completely fresh (still in the sacs and fertilised, which is different than say caviar which is unfertilised). It is totally without smell when super fresh and very easy to eat raw. I also freeze extra, though it isn't as nice once thawed.

Raw, fresh roe looks like this:

Sometimes it's more white/gray and egg size varies from teensy-tiny to small little balls, depending on the fish you're eating it from. ### Melinda — 2012-08-03T05:46:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42634>

Lovely pictures but show me what's inside, that big thing is not a roe, I think. Isn't it little round things that are roe?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-03T07:29:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42638>

> **Melinda;42906 wrote:** Lovely pictures but show me what's inside, that big thing is not a roe, I think. Isn't it little round things that are roe?

The picture I posted is exactly what you'd buy if you bought fresh roe, unprocessed in any way. All you are seeing is a translucent veiny sac that holds the roe inside. The roe itself is hard to see because unless it's very large fish, the eggs are very tiny. Here is a picture that shows you when they are cut

. This is smoked roe (it's typically smoked in the sac), but raw looks the same... it just isn't as firm as smoked.

### Melinda — 2012-08-03T16:05:09+0300

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-04T00:08:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-42769>

> **bigknitwit;42959 wrote:** So, assuming I can find the fresh roe, and I wasn't yet brave enough to partake of it raw, how would I cook it? What does it taste like?

Well, i don't cook it.  I cut it in bite-sized pieces and swallow it down. However, I have cooked it and it's simple. You can just put it in a pan with some oil for a minute or two on each side, then it's firm enough to cut. Then you can cook as you do fish. I find it tastes very fishy when cooked. It tastes of very little when raw, just a sea-taste.

Believe me, mine have everything removed! I eat them but do not love them. I am learning to love them. I will love them!

### kathylu — 2012-08-05T04:22:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43002>

> **Destiny;43245 wrote:** Soul, you mean the poop in the raw ones, right? The smoked ones don't have it there?? ... I hope.

Since oysters are filter feeders they are constantly pumping a stream of water through the digestive tract...so they are fairly clean. If you buy oysters, clams and mussels live you can let them soak in salt water for 30 minutes before you cook them and they will blow out any gunk....

### Destiny — 2012-08-05T13:50:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43025>

That might be a possibility ..... down the road.

### chocolate — 2012-08-07T02:52:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43327>

I was just looking through the cupboards for what to put together for dinner and found some cans of straw mushrooms. They are medicinal, so they have the beta glucans. They are dirt cheap, too. Neat.

[http://www.scirp.org/journal/PaperInformation.aspx?paperID=20512&publishStatus=2](http://http://www.scirp.org/journal/PaperInformation.aspx?paperID=20512&publishStatus=2)

"The Comparison of the Effect of Oat and Shiitake Mushroom Powder to Prevent Body Weight Gain in Rats Fed High Fat Diet,"

> This study demonstrated that the preventing obesity via lowering plasma TAG and fat deposition was different depending on beta-glucan origin, either from oats and Shiitake mushroom.

These are shiitakes, but its pretty interesting how much they charge for oats versus mushrooms. Its about the same for "good" oats.

Do you know how much water this would save?

### LinD — 2012-08-07T04:19:11+0300

I'm in awe what many of you are doing.

Will you offer any starter pointers to us newbies? While I grew up in New Orleans and had lots of craw fish, crab, & shrimp as a kid, I now eat cooked fish of all kinds, plus shrimp. I've only had oysters a couple of times, but it was a very long time ago.

Thanks!

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-07T05:11:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43359>

few months ago... I still hate the taste of the frozen ones. I just choke them down. Can't be worse than a MHS. Oysters are kind of necessary for the zinc....

I'd say if you want to start eating stuff you think is gross, you kinda need to start at the thought first.... focus your thinking on all the good stuff that it will do to your body and block things like "gross" or "not tasty".

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-07T05:40:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43367>

My roommate found out today.... about the fish heads. And the whole shrimp. It was ..... interesting  I just said want to fix a brain, eat a brain, you know?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-07T05:58:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43369>

> **LinD;43649 wrote:** Cool. I kinda expected this kind of reply, LOL.
>
>
>
> But really, what do you do? Say, take mackerel head or fish(es?) with LOTS of lemon or lime juice and use Braun whizzer? (Actually, we have a new one that is tons stronger.... From Costco, of choose.) Any ice? Not too much, right? I don't know. Just asking for details, ok! Thanks.
>
>
>
> With all that said, I don't know if I'll get brave to do this on my own or not... Maybe if my husband is out playing golf. Otherwise he'll think I've gone mad. Anyone in the D/FW area?

You can totally do it LinD. My husband did think I was crazy when he first saw me doing it. But now it's routine for the kids and him to watch me make my 'smoothies.' No ice for me. But my fish is frozen (I use the whole thing except the very end that's just 'tail'... waiting to hear in B-G 40 how important fish tails are and then I'll reconsider). I thaw the fish for a few minutes on the counter (my blender appreciates that) and then chop, throw it into the blender, and do what Soul says. I've been adding lemon juice, sea salt and some chlorella if I've got it. Just enough extra water to be able to blend. And I drink without the straw (and with strong green tea or red wine on hand to swish in my mouth afterwards). It's really not nearly as bad as you think. And you'll feel great. Or at least you think you will. And there's not really a difference

### LinD — 2012-08-07T06:01:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43370>

I find Inger's recipe online:

Take raw fresh mackerelheads, cut one head into maybe 6 pieces with skissors, directly into your blender / mixer. Add water to get nice consistence, that you can drink it. Mix until smooth. Enjoy.\*\*

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-07T20:29:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43473>

Lin, I have used the sardines and Pompono (or something like that). Like Kiwi said, whatever is fresh. The store had some marckerel but the eyes were cloudy so no go. I just cut the fish up when I get home repackage it into chunks that my blender can handle (KitchenAid) and free it in one serving sizes. When ready to have a smoothie I take out the package, let it thaw for about 20 min and throw the whole thing in the blender with water and lemon juice. I'm still at the lots of lemon juice stage to kill the taste. The pompono fish was much better than the sardines. Sardines seem way fishy to me and these were fresh ones. It sure makes for a nice dinner.

There is no such thing as a generic "serving size". That's something that CW pulled out of their behinds. A serving is what satisfies your hunger. It's 2 sardines one morning and 3 the next and maybe 5 for dinner after you spent the day climbing mountains. And whatever else you might want to eat.

Inger, I've been doing the whole fishies, also. I think you just got a couple bad ones the other day.... I'm fine with them. Gets you the liver and all the K2 that's in the organs  But if I could just get fish heads I'd do that, too.... but I'm too cheap (or broke ) to throw out the left overs.

Diary = acne.... for me.

Let's begin the race for Optimal! May I introduce the Optimized Living Team..... who wants to be part?

I got Inger, Kiwi and Nonchalant. And, obviously, I  Rolling towards optimal!

Not sure about the face.... started a few days ago.... just - changing. Clear skin. Clear eyes (used to be dryish). Cheekbones showing for a change. I look more alert. Not so tired and off. My lips are redder and my face is more tan.... noticing lots of things. If now my jaw muscles would go away a bit..... too much carrot chewing during raw/vegan.....lol.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-08T21:14:06+0300

> You're my MHS sista after all!

Alas, I must confess, I've only had the one Inger's Cocktail. I went back to the store a couple weeks later, but didn't like the looks of the mackerel. So I checked the sardines, and didn't like them either. Eyes didn't look fresh. So I bought some frozen green-lipped clams and some flying fish eggs. But then I saw that the eggs were "seasoned" whatever that meant. So I've tried to soak them, but I'm probably dosing myself with something bad.

But I've been doing very, very well on almost daily cerviche, using small sea scallops and various other ingredients. Oh, and I've discovered raw oyster!

Anyway, I plan on going back to the store tomorrow. Gotta have some more of that enlightenment/focus/ambition/empowerment! And lemon! They had better have some awesome fish this time.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-09T01:19:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43771>

> **Souldanzer;43939 wrote:** I do something similar.... I get an image going that this is the miracle medicine for my brain.... and then it goes down easily. I forgot to add the lemon juice today and my smoothie was loaded with dulse.... had to imagine intensely

Thanks for inviting me onto the Optimal Team. It's a pleasure to be here! I am nowhere near you in the Inger Cocktail department... I do one smoothie every other day for dinner. I do raw roe daily and figure when that's out of season I'll up the IC's to daily. I can't imagine well enough to make them turn into creamy chocolate... but I literally think with every mouthful about how much this is nurturing me and giving me. That mindset-change really makes all the difference. I can feel grateful as I drink. (It also helps that I saved up for a new blender so now they are smooth again and I don't have to eat them, lumps and bits poking out, with a spoon! )

Like Soul and Inger, I feel this has made a world of difference to me and my energy and outlook.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-09T05:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43807>

> **Cavemam;44092 wrote:** Why would you need Chocolate? I hear it tastes lemony and lovely! Soul, where are you getting the mackrels? I'm about ready to dive in. I've got vacation next week - but when I come back I'm gonna hit that MHS, omg I swear. I should put this in my calendar and pretend it's a client.

You can get mackerels at WFM. They don't have guts though. Maybe you could ask for them to be whole. I find them too big for my blender and have pieces of spine in there that won't break up. So now I buy at the Asian store, says wild caught and I yell at them if they try to take out the guts. It can really be any fish. I really like herring and smaller mackerels and sardines but have done tiny little silverfish and smelts, too.

I'd say either do the lemon or the chocolate

### Glamazon — 2012-08-09T05:35:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43813>

Just got back from WF and bought two huge frozen salmon heads. (1.99 a pound) the heads were 2 pounds each. I think I will be making stock or soup from them this weekend. I asked them if they have salmon skin and they said always. Just call ahead and tell them when I want to pick it up and they will save it for me. As the heads were sitting on the counter at the store I couldnt help thinking "no way those are going to fit in the blender; they are bigger than soft balls." I am not ready for smoothie time yet. Anybody have any good stock or head recipe they want to share?

$1.99/lb is a very cheap for a high quality nutrition bomb for sure. Eating optimal on a budget is possible. They said they will always have bones if I just call ahead.

### caroline — 2012-08-09T05:59:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43818>

> **Glamazon;44104 wrote:** Just got back from WF and bought two huge frozen salmon heads. (1.99 a pound) the heads were 2 pounds each. I think I will be making stock or soup from them this weekend. I asked them if they have salmon skin and they said always. Just call ahead and tell them when I want to pick it up and they will save it for me. As the heads were sitting on the counter at the store I couldnt help thinking "no way those are going to fit in the blender; they are bigger than soft balls." I am not ready for smoothie time yet. Anybody have any good stock or head recipe they want to share?
>
>
>
> $1.99/lb is a very cheap for a high quality nutrition bomb for sure. Eating optimal on a budget is possible. They said they will always have bones if I just call ahead.

I make a shrimp chowder with the shells so I would just use the salmon heads instead.

I gently saute some leeks and carrots and celery and shallots - don't brown. Add salmon head and water and maybe a lite chicken stock and simmer for an hour or so. Then strain it and when ready to make soup - start again by sauteing the veg you want and add fish stock and simmer until veg are cooked and then puree. I have been thinking of adding a sweet potato to thicken. Some people are saying to simmer fish heads for 12 hours or more - I personally think this is way too long but see what you think. Let me know how it goes.

Necessary, I'm sure not. But I want the WHOLE thing. I use smaller fish (typically sardines) and it all grinds up perfectly. I'm happy with my lemon flavour, Soul. I'll save my chocolate for dessert!

### Inger — 2012-08-09T08:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-43838>

> **caroline;44106 wrote:** Is it necessary to have the guts???? you are killing me here! My fish guy said he will scale and clean sardines for me -but I know I want the liver - but the guts??? I am still trying to get past the eyeballs and teeth.

Caroline.. I have to confess.. the guts are the hardest part for me too. Yuck. But blended up I take everything. Everything. I just hold my nose and choke down, thats it - it cannot get easier.

I just had a big bowl of whole small freshwaterfishes cooked in fishbroth for breakfast, guts head and all. Thats a little yucky too but it does not taste bad! It rather looks yucky. So I actually enjoyed it a little, and do not need to hold my nose at all. They are very small fishes, about as big as my finger. I guess a very healthy meal too even when not raw. And these wildcaught sweetwater-fishes are dirt cheap! Around 1,50 € for one pound! And I get all the goodness, heads guts and all...

I see it as a continuum, and the further down you are, the more you need the raw seafood.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-15T23:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-45079>

Yesterday I ate more than usual for my lunch, because it tasted so fantastic (yeah, it was cerviche), and I was surprised at my full tummy. Surprised because I haven't had a full stomach in a while. I usually eat some, and if I still want more, I eat something else, and continue until I don't want any more.

If it isn't a full tummy telling us to stop, what is? While eating, I get the definite impression that I've had enough. So I stop. But my stomach might feel a bit empty still, and I tell it to stop complaining. I know I've eaten enough.

Somehow the brain is keeping track of what is being eaten, and isn't necessarily relying on signals from the stomach. At least not the distended, full stomach signal. If I eat nutrient-dense food, I'm usually going to stop before full. I remember back in the old days, sometimes I waited impatiently for my stomach to digest, just so I could eat some more.

### PrimalPam — 2012-08-16T02:14:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-45106>

> **Nonchalant;45411 wrote:** Yesterday I ate more than usual for my lunch, because it tasted so fantastic (yeah, it was cerviche), and I was surprised at my full tummy. Surprised because I haven't had a full stomach in a while. I usually eat some, and if I still want more, I eat something else, and continue until I don't want any more.
>
>
>
> If it isn't a full tummy telling us to stop, what is? While eating, I get the definite impression that I've had enough. So I stop. But my stomach might feel a bit empty still, and I tell it to stop complaining. I know I've eaten enough.
>
>
>
> Somehow the brain is keeping track of what is being eaten, and isn't necessarily relying on signals from the stomach. At least not the distended, full stomach signal. If I eat nutrient-dense food, I'm usually going to stop before full. I remember back in the old days, sometimes I waited impatiently for my stomach to digest, just so I could eat some more.

I find the exact same phenomenon. I used to eat to the point of being stuffed, then I'd wait so I could stuff more in.

But these days, I eat, then I stop. Then I realize, I could eat more, but I'm like...no, I'm good. Who is this girl and what have you done with my old appetite?? lol

Today, I ate half the salmon I laid out for dinner. Just was good. I wrapped up the other half for tomorrow night. It was so delicious, but I was like, I feel good. And once it gets past about 6:00 pm for me, I'm done even if I start feeling hunger. Because these days, the hunger feelings go away through the night and I'm good till BAB.

I think it's because we're eating so nutrient-dense. For me, every single day now includes D supplements, a Brazil nut, CO, Green Tea, usually fish, etc. Plus, are you doing CT? I'm doing 5 x/week, so I think all of the above have led to leptin being released from fat cells and simply working properly.

### PrimalPam — 2012-08-16T02:17:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-45107>

Also, I am now watching the PaleoFX DVD's (I attended! But could not get to all the sessions).

Nora Gedgaudkas (sp.?) had a very simplistic way to look at it: She talked about all the feelings we have just prior to eating, when we're eating SAD: cranky, tired, listless, etc. When we're done? Stuffed, tired, bloated.

But when you're eating full-on Paleo (or epi-Paleo), what should you feel prior to eating? Hungry. When you're done eating? Not hungry.

### SeaHorse — 2012-08-16T17:17:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-45200>

> Do you just cook it like any other seafood? Never had squid before. Think it's d\*sgusting. But I'm gonna try it anyways

I used to cook squid in Newfoundland when we lived there....it can get really tough and rubbery with too high a heat. A local recipe I remember was to stuff it with bread crumbs and savoury and bake it...sometimes it worked and sometimes it didn't. You could stuff it with crab and herbs to keep things more moist and without grains. Maybe with lots of ghee or CO to make a sauce and keep it from rubberizing.

There's always a quick stirfry with garlic and herbs once you cut it up a bit. A cousin of my husband's never recovered from finding out that the "onion" rings he was eating at a Chinese restaurant were squid. He had been enjoying them until he was enlightened....silly man.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-25T19:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-47055>

Dopamine is a chemical found naturally in the brain as I mention in the webinar and in the cycloset thread. It is also found in the serum of blood. It acts directly on nerves and it can also act as a hormone in many tissues to give many secondary affects. When given intravenously, it is in a class of drugs known as vasopressors. These drugs increase the blood pressure dramatically. So when you take a drug that affects the dopamine level in the brain it is not uncommon to see many changes in BP. Cycloset is a very low dose form of bromocriptine so it usually affects gut more than BP.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-25T19:47:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-47058>

There's nothing quite like trying to explain to a nurse that there are measurements on the side of the cuffs for a REASON...

Lol....may I make fun about the two of us once again  They used to have to track down a child-size cuff for me and still couldn't really find a reading.

"White Coat Syndrome" is actually pretty common. It's usually just a few points but for me it's quite a difference. So while they "believe" me that it's lower at home, they still have to record the actual reading. Some doctors will have you bring in your home-readings to look at them. I brought in my wrist-BP monitor that stores the readings with date and time. So they now believe me and also have an idea about how freaked out I really get at the doc's.

Ask them to take you BP at the end of your appointment. Sometimes that can help for it to normalize.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-25T19:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-47061>

> **Jack Kruse;47429 wrote:** I eat a boatload of them......maybe too many

Avocados? I've been having such a hard time with the no sat fat with seafood. But avos as pudding has been doing the trick sometimes and I will actually stay away from drowning my precious DHA in a can of coconut milk.

### BJK77 — 2012-08-25T19:56:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-47062>

> **Jack Kruse;47429 wrote: I eat a boatload of them......maybe too many
>
>
> Please don't ever tell me there is such a thing as too many avocados!
>
>
>
> Endless;47426 wrote:** I have been eating avocado with it, but how do you cook it? Do you use any oils, or just water?

I just eat them raw with my seafood. My favorite meal right now is scallops and shrimp sauteed with bacon. I cut up some avocado, put the seafood on top and drizzle the bacon grease over it all. I add a small amount of bone broth to the pan with the bacon grease and simmer it down til it becomes a nice, thick consistency - almost like bacon jam. Eat a piece of seafood, bacon and avocado with each bite topped with the bacon jam- it's the most amazing combination!

I also sprinkle on a little kelp and dulse for good measure!

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-25T19:57:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-47063>

> **Jack Kruse;47479 wrote:** Dopamine is a chemical found naturally in the brain as I mention in the webinar and in the cycloset thread. It is also found in the serum of blood. It acts directly on nerves and it can also act as a hormone in many tissues to give many secondary affects. When given intravenously, it is in a class of drugs known as vasopressors. These drugs increase the blood pressure dramatically. So when you take a drug that affects the dopamine level in the brain it is not uncommon to see many changes in BP. Cycloset is a very low dose form of bromocriptine so it usually affects gut more than BP.

now I'm really curious what the l-dopa they just added to my NT program is going to do to me..... I like having a BP around 100/60 or even a bit lower. It's where I feel best. Calm.

### ealachan — 2012-08-25T21:05:17+0300

It acts directly on nerves and it can also act as a hormone in many tissues to give many secondary affects. When given intravenously, it is in a class of drugs known as vasopressors. These drugs increase the blood pressure dramatically. So when you take a drug that affects the dopamine level in the brain it is not uncommon to see many changes in BP. Cycloset is a very low dose form of bromocriptine so it usually affects gut more than BP.

I wonder if this is why the bupropion (Wellbutrin) that I was on for years was jacking my blood pressure up? Bupropion partially blocks dopamine reuptake. Less reuptake = less clearing of dopamine from the system = more circulating dopamine, right? Or did I understand that wrong?

In another thread I forgot to mention other types of oils I like...and sounded boring.

I like sesame oil for flavouring. Mac nut oil sounds wonderful. Avacado oil is nice too. Olive oil is the stand-by affordable MUFA oil. haha. Bacon grease, yum! Is duck fat good MUFA too? Am assuming b/c ducks are aquatic birds... So yes, different MUFA type oils make a good variety for fish/seafood dishes.

See, when throughout history and people say sat fat is bad, but then conflicting info. on olive oil etc. Jack makes this crystal clear and easy to understand. So now I eat whatever fat is natural to that product I'm eating. Sat fat with land animals, and MUFA with seafood. hehe.

So I should go back and apologize to my dad for cooking his ackee and salt fish with olive oil...lol. Now I know...LOL (But his mother used CO oil...) So ya...lol.

Tip for eating at restaurant. I now only order something seafood. I feel the 'safest' (as in quality of the food in a restaurant setting). For example, usually they are not going to have grass-fed meat...and chicken is "..." so fish or seafood...it's the easiest thing.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-08-30T17:07:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-48360>

Grrrr...I'm still not on 29.5 day cycles yet...sigh.

I got my cycle in July on day...'gazillion and one' (well like 3 months. So I was EXCITED for that and ya only a 'slight' discomfort the night before...but it felt more like a little diarhea.....then next day BAM period. I was jumping through the roof. It was followed by like ZERO pain and I didn't need ANY kind of pill for cramps either. :-D

Here I should have had it arrive again on Aug. 19th, but it didn't. I think(?) I had spotting on Aug. 11. I've been feeling pretty good otherwise...as in AMAZING...so I don't know why it's taking it's time....too much estrogen still? (Even though I'm not suffereing typical signs of too much estrogen).

I know my ratio is off...I just know it is....But I don't think it's that the progesterone is too 'low' per se...but rather that it's only a little on the low side, but estrogen might still be too dominating. (Despite no more swollen breasts and excess in hips/butt).

I just keep telling myself my hormones are still 'sorting themselves out'. I'm going to buy some more colostrum today, since I really noticed benefits from taking that (and ran out about 2 weeks ago). I will also be diligent with maca this time...like actually stick to taking it for at least a month. I never seemed to follow through with that one...don't know why.

I will stick to my light cycles and seafood and that in itself is beneficial no matter what!

Pffft....my hormonal panel will be better in my 30's than in my 20's, go figure. :-D When I reach menopause I have the mindset that I will not suffer because I am building up my 'immunity' from now. Sorting out hormones now, make sure my adrenals are happy and ready for their 'extra' work during that time. Make sure my brain is working....etc.

Congrats though to everyone who is getting cycles based on the moon! See you on the other side...SOON!  (Getting there).

>
>
> I haven't had a cycle since February and before that it was October 2011!

I guess I shouldn't complain about 3 months apart then...hmmm. However ya, I'm 27 and have NOT had kids yet, (and want some) at least in my 30's. My sex drive is good though...with feeling horny enough times usually at night before bed and in the morning. I went for a period of time of not feeling horny at all...it sucked. That was during the 3 months of no cycle. So since July 21st this has improved even though no actual menstration in August. That's why I have so much hope, b/c I know my body is sorting itself out.

Things that help for me personally (so far) after much experimentation over the last 8 months...:

\-EPI-PALEO (seafood, fats 'right fats with right foods', fermented veg etc.)

\-circadian rhythm: bed time no later than 10pm (and I read in low yellow light before that...)\* wake up with sunrise and gaze at the sun. eat breakfast within half an hour to an hour (although with the sun gazing I feel this is not AS important than it would be in winter, since in winter/dark I'd be relying more on the protein fat as the circadian cue, whereas right now I am using the sun for that

\-colostrum (I want to try goat colostrum next, but the bovine stuff seemed really good too)

\-maca (I never am consistent with it...so this time I am going to be diligent for a whole month. I bought some yesterday and took today...I do think it's beneficial for me).

\-fermented drinks when possible

\-BP coffee (although currently I've been putting only CO in it...I need to switch to ghee or something, but not sure if that was what was causing me grief in June....b/c something went wonky in June...)

\-MOVING!!!!!!!!!!!! I am no longer in a stressful situation after a year. Yay! That probably helps A LOT!

\*Note about circadian cycles: At the moment I'm visiting with my parents for the last 2 weeks. The thing I can't do that is appropriate is stop eating ~4 hours before bedtime. I need to eat dinner with them (and they eat WAY too late) out of respect before I move away and don't see them for a while. So it's out of respect... I am definitely looking forward to eating dinner at an earlier time again once in Ottawa. (Yay, Ottawa!).

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-08-31T16:59:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-48568>

O, I don't mean to say that the 3 month abscence was the only time of irregular cycle...My cycles have always been very long and irregular. Last winter really sucked because it was clearly a problem with estrogen dominance with the swelling boobs, excess hips/ass and thighs, acne, cramping bloating for weeks before any period, PMS (again for weeks before any period), depressed, like 0 dopamine or something, weight gain only at cycle but didn't lose afterwards (it's a REALLY gross kind of fat that feels like a tumor), etc.

Ok, I notice that with the type of subcutaneous fat that I have right now it's REALLY nice kind...like it's a. evenly distributed b. I still have muscular definition c. It's 'soft, but not flabby' as in like a smoothness to the skin...or supple. not sure.

I mean I didn't think I would get excited about having 'fat'...but I feel like there is something 'different' about this fat...and that it's not 'pathological'. I didn't know you could have 'fat' but still be 'defined'. Weird. I think there is even improvement in my legs which traditionally are ....you know...

It's weird how inflammation/gut problems affect different people, like some people it will attack them primarily metabolically whereas other people it will affect them more in the brain. Of course, it's both...but I mean in terms of presentation of symptoms.

Ok, I'm rambling now. hehe.

\*I was about to post long life bio...deleted it b/c don't want to waste people's time\* lol

### SeaHorse — 2012-08-31T19:29:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-48593>

I stopped having my period when I was 45...eight years ago...thought is was the best thing ever, except now I realize it was also a sign of hormone problems my whole life....waiting to get going with BHRT now. I had both our girls in my thirties 33 and 39, so I was crashing and burning during that time...not fun. Last night I asked JK's opinion on needing to have periods with BHRT and he didn't think it was nec...I'm relieved about that. I don't miss them one little bit...Now, I'm even remember what having a sex drive feels like just by the ep-paleo and ct and light...bring on the BHRT a.s.a.p and more seafood!

My sex drive is good though...with feeling horny enough times usually at night before bed and in the morning. I went for a period of time of not feeling horny at all...it sucked. That was during the 3 months of no cycle. So since July 21st this has improved even though no actual menstration in August. That's why I have so much hope, b/c I know my body is sorting itself out.
>

>

>
> \-EPI-PALEO (seafood, fats 'right fats with right foods', fermented veg etc.)
>
> \-circadian rhythm: bed time no later than 10pm (and I read in low yellow light before that...)\* wake up with sunrise and gaze at the sun. eat breakfast within half an hour to an hour (although with the sun gazing I feel this is not AS important than it would be in winter, since in winter/dark I'd be relying more on the protein fat as the circadian cue, whereas right now I am using the sun for that
>
> \-colostrum (I want to try goat colostrum next, but the bovine stuff seemed really good too)
>

>

>
> \-BP coffee (although currently I've been putting only CO in it...I need to switch to ghee or something, but not sure if that was what was causing me grief in June....b/c something went wonky in June...)

You're definitely giving me hope here  Sometimes I feel like my body is starting to sort itself out, but it inevitably crashes again. When I first started supplementing with DHEA I was actually getting a sex drive!! It was the first time in 8 years - I can remember exactly because the last time I truly had a drive was when I was pregnant with my son - holy wow!! Can't wait to get that back again  Funny thing was it wasn't a strong libido that led to me getting pregnant even. I just wanted another baby. Sex was just the vehicle to make it happen. I always knew my hormones were off, but my docs never took me seriously. Every single idiot I saw just wanted to throw me on the pill. Thankfully I never took it for long. It always made me extremely nauseous after the first few days, so I stopped taking it and learned to not voice my concerns.

I've been thinking about trying colostrum, but I thought Dr. K only suggests human and I can't go there for multiple reasons. I do have access to local, organic, grass-fed bovine colostrum. Maybe I'll pick some up when I visit my local farm next week.

What exactly do you take the maca for? I bought some spur of the moment a few months ago and it's been sitting in my cupboard ever since. I don't think I'm E dominant. My hormones are pretty much nonexistent - will it help that?

I'm also hitting the epi-paleo diet and circadian cycles HARD, so hopefully I'll start to see some benefits from my efforts there.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-09-01T15:50:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-48709>

> **Souldanzer;49146 wrote:** Squid as ceviche.... fail. Don't go there

I tried it..but it was sliced already for me...so I liked it. But some don't like the rubbery feel...hmmm...I love squid!

Speaking of squid. I met a 72 yr old lady...VERY good skin with slight wrinkles on her forehead (like DUH of course you'll have some...) but her skin had a suppleness to it. She said she was a bit tired and I asked her how her sleep was she said it was ''good'...so anyways it led into conversation about how old she is etc. She thought it was 'good genes' for her health. Continued the convo as I nod my head in agreement. Then I was like...hmm...is one of your secrets seafood? She said YES!!! She said she grew up in Portugal and lived by the sea....she ate/eats all seafoods of all types. I told her that I started eating seafood too etc. Anyways I said...but you know I eat stuff most people gag at...for example SQUID. She looked in delight and exclaimed "I LOVE squid!!!" She then proceeded to tell of a recipe she uses to cook squid.

Sometimes the world is good! :-D

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-09-01T16:00:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-48711>

> **BJK77;49154 wrote:** You're definitely giving me hope here  Sometimes I feel like my body is starting to sort itself out, but it inevitably crashes again. When I first started supplementing with DHEA I was actually getting a sex drive!! It was the first time in 8 years - I can remember exactly because the last time I truly had a drive was when I was pregnant with my son - holy wow!! Can't wait to get that back again  Funny thing was it wasn't a strong libido that led to me getting pregnant even. I just wanted another baby. Sex was just the vehicle to make it happen. I always knew my hormones were off, but my docs never took me seriously. Every single idiot I saw just wanted to throw me on the pill. Thankfully I never took it for long. It always made me extremely nauseous after the first few days, so I stopped taking it and learned to not voice my concerns.
>
>
>
> I've been thinking about trying colostrum, but I thought Dr. K only suggests human and I can't go there for multiple reasons. I do have access to local, organic, grass-fed bovine colostrum. Maybe I'll pick some up when I visit my local farm next week.
>
>
>
> What exactly do you take the maca for? I bought some spur of the moment a few months ago and it's been sitting in my cupboard ever since. I don't think I'm E dominant. My hormones are pretty much nonexistent - will it help that?
>
>
>
> I'm also hitting the epi-paleo diet and circadian cycles HARD, so hopefully I'll start to see some benefits from my efforts there.

Hey, well I know Dr. K said only human colostrum...but I felt good effects even from bovine. I prefer powder form, not capsules. Also goat colostrum apparently has even closer structure to human colostrum than bovine. I haven't tried that, since I'm in search of that atm.

Maca is not for E dominance...in fact many ppl use it for menopause relief. lol What it does is it acts on the hypothalamaus, pituitary and adrenals. So it helps the body (supposed to ) to make it's own hormones more efficiently. I mean I know I have my ovaries doing their job etc. and I'm only 27, but I think the HPA axis could use a little help. Sometimes ovaries can only do so much of the work, you know?

Glad you're doing epi-paleo and abiding by circadian cycles...honestly that's one of the greatest things I got to learn in my life b/c of this blog. In regards to seaweed, remember that the iodine rich sources are supposed to balance out P:E2 ratios (even if you're not E dominant per se....the ratio could be a little skewed...who knows w/o testing).

Pffft I've come a long way with this...and I feel the majority was all in the last month! Like from March to July...working...I don't know. Maybe that was just the adjustment phase. Who knows. But now I feel like I'm on my way to Optimal for SURE this time! I'm pretty sure (?) I ovulated this month...so that is good. We'll see if I get period in ~ 2 weeks. lol I hope my move doesn't disturb anything. lol (It's a positive move though...so it should be OK).

Ooooooo and no more bleeding from the rectum...WOO HOO! (lol sorry for TMI).

> **ealachan;51270 wrote:** Cod have tongues?! Well I'll be darned. Learn something new every day around here...

They are good cooked in lots of butter. The tongue is really not a tongue but a fatty thingy found on the bottom of the head

I found a recipe [http://awickedscoff.blogspot.com/2011/09/cod-tongues-and-tarter-sauce.html](http://http://awickedscoff.blogspot.com/2011/09/cod-tongues-and-tarter-sauce.html)

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-27T00:08:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-54584>

> **ealachan;51270 wrote:** Cod have tongues?! Well I'll be darned. Learn something new every day around here...

I've been able to get whole cod a few times...the tongues and cheeks are considered a delicacy in Newfoundland...served up with scrunchions which are little bits of fat pork crisped up...yum yum and very epi-paleo. If only they didn't add mounds of french fries it would all be great. There's a street in St. John's Newfoundland that is nicknamed Grease Alley because of all the fish and chip shops. Sometimes you could get tongues and cheeks there, most of the larger supermarkets had tongues regularly...I'm getting teary eyed here so I have to stop thinking about all that fresh fish I left behind.

### caroline — 2012-09-27T12:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-54715>

Sometimes you could get tongues and cheeks there, most of the larger supermarkets had tongues regularly...I'm getting teary eyed here so I have to stop thinking about all that fresh fish I left behind.

My husband was born and raised in Nova Scotia. He always talked about cod cheeks and tongues. I thought he was being silly. Who is the silly one now?

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-27T17:40:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-diet-everything-goes.2806/post-54748>

I think to be optimal....they should have served the eyes and liver in the same dish!

# Thread: Full moon stuff
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-08-02T04:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-42433>

The moon phases simply must have some influences and some circadian relevance. When out looking at it last night, i realized it did have lots of blue light. I wonder what lessons there are to learn.

### ColdBren — 2012-08-02T06:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-42450>

Hmmmm, interesting.....I wonder about its effects on our fluid and hormones too

### Melinda — 2012-08-02T06:45:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-42455>

I'm also noticing the blueness of the moon and stars. I peek at the night sky when I walk the dog before bed with my orange glasses on. A few months ago I raised the curtains in my bedroom to enjoy the moon light while I slept. Now I figure I should think like I'm sleeping deep in the cave and keep the shades down. Not sure what is optimal.

### freshveggies — 2012-08-02T16:52:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-42497>

The full moom affects my sleep in a negative way. The underground water veins get larger during that time.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-02T17:14:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-42503>

Ok..this makes me wonder.... I haven't been sleeping well and waking up btwn 2-4am (not normal) - but could it be the moon? we do sleep in a dark room at night

one things for sure TOTM threw off my sleep and oxytocin bedtime prep! Back on track now!

### youthfuliz — 2012-08-02T18:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-42521>

In the old times women's cycles were guided by the moon - at that time, full moon was mid cycle for all women, time to be active and have sex!  That's why full moon is always romantic and makes us feel want to hug someone.

I learnt this from the book _Sex, Lies and Menopause_.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-02T19:01:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-42524>

yes...we'll my cycle has never synced w/the moon - add in BHRT and I'm no where near the normal cycle - though I'm getting closer and closer to a 28day cycle!

### ealachan — 2012-08-02T19:05:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-42525>

> **youthfuliz;42786 wrote:** In the old times women's cycles were guided by the moon - at that time, full moon was mid cycle for all women, time to be active and have sex!  That's why full moon is always romantic and makes us feel want to hug someone.
>
>
>
> I learnt this from the book _Sex, Lies and Menopause_.

Was there any actual scientific data to back that assertion up? I'm not trying to be snotty...I'm just curious how anyone could even begin to go about trying to prove something like that.

### youthfuliz — 2012-08-02T19:50:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-42528>

I don't know... but I guess if someone wants to make the connection between hormones and lunar cycle it shouldn't be difficult. Just like summer is differnt from winter ( I assume summer time is the fun sexy time within a year  - I think Jack hinted on this in his blogs) then full moon is different from new moon. Everything is guided by circadian rhythm!

### chocolate — 2012-08-06T20:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-43246>

I was just looking this up. The article is pretty comprehensive, right down to the phase of the moon on your birth and menstrual fertility.OY!

[http://www.epigee.org/menstruation\_lunar\_fertility.html](http://http://www.epigee.org/menstruation_lunar_fertility.html)

> Not surprisingly, Dr. Jonas was met with quite a bit of skepticism from the medical and scientific world. Since his theory cannot be proven by modern-day scientific reasoning, many choose to dismiss the idea of having two fertile times as nonsense. However, a British **study done on lunar phase fertility found that men's sperm counts significantly increased during their lunar fertility period.** Another study showed that women who practiced abstinence during both their biological and lunar fertile period were 98% effective in preventing pregnancy. This is a pretty significant increase from the 75% effectiveness rate of being abstinent only during your biological fertility period.

I guess that's their birthday, or the phase of the moon. I wonder if guys are being treated for low sperm counts when they are normal.

### youthfuliz — 2012-08-06T21:00:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-43261>

Interesting... Just checked, my birthday lunar phase is exactly the same as the 14th day of my cycle this month! Fun stuff!

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-20T17:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon-stuff.2811/post-338683>

All laid out here ladies. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/110412109](https://www.patreon.com/posts/110412109)

# Thread: Craving the Sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2012-08-02T20:56:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/post-42543>

Abut a month ago, I started getting at least 1 hour of sun a day on sunny days. Most days I get about 2 hours.

I never burned and have developed a nice tan. What is interesting is that I find I crave the sun. I go out during the hottest part of the day and work in the garden. I quit after and hour or 2 because I am getting tired, not because the sun is too hot. If it clouds up, I quit working and go inside until the sun comes back out.

I am guessing this is because I finally have my vitamin d above 70. It could also be because of CT.

Anyone else find they crave direct sun?

### raysblue — 2012-08-02T21:25:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/post-42547>

If you lived in England you would realise we all are craving for it during another terrible summer.

Since following paelo and Dr Kruse's protocol I do not use sun cream and tan a very nice bronze colour, like I used to do as a child...not sure why! But sun in the summer and cold in the winter is now a craving. Need to board a plane to somewhere warm.

### kathylu — 2012-08-03T03:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/post-42602>

I can't say I'm actually craving the sun, but since my D is 75 now...I don't need sun screen anymore! Whoo Hoo! I am not really tanning either (maybe a little), but I can stay out for several hours without burning. This is an actual miracle, cuz I'm naturally zombie white.

### Huck — 2012-08-03T03:57:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/post-42620>

I started getting direct sun to try to raise testosterone. Maybe it is working and that's why I enjoy getting out in the sun so much. Or maybe it is topping off my vitamin c. Just had blood drawn this morning for labs, so should know in a few days if the sun has had any effect.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-03T05:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/post-42635>

the reason you can tolerate the sun more is your DHEA and Vitamin D ratios rise when you eat the way you are designed too

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-03T06:32:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/post-42636>

I got burned yesterday.... in my face  Not bad but still red today. And my D is above 100 and am taking DHEA. And I already got a bit of a tan going this year.....

It was a bit cloudy and I lost track of time.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-03T15:43:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/post-42659>

I've been driving with the window down, at least when the sun is on that side of the car. In 100+ temps. Air conditioning on full blast, of course. Arm out the window, and leaning my head a bit so that it gets more of the sun. I just love being able to tolerate its heat. Amazing what you can do when you aren't burning up with so much inflammation inside.

### tweety — 2012-08-04T02:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/post-42793>

Yes- why after a summer of no sunscreen and no burns, can you then go out one day for the same amount of time, and burn? What gives? I have noticed this too. Nothing but tan, until two weeks ago. Now I have a little burn, and it is taingk forever to go away.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-04T03:07:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-the-sun.2819/post-42794>

mine's turned into tan today.....

# Thread: Classic Pregnenolone Steal?

**Posts:** 17

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-04T03:23:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/classic-pregnenolone-steal.2847/post-42796>

Here are 2 sets of cortisol labs. They got so much worse after I thought I was doing all the right things. I started supplementing DHEA & pregnenolone in June, so about 8 weeks before the second set of labs. I am taking 20mg DHEA & 200mg Pregnenolone. I have been avoiding artificial light at night and have put up room darkening shades. I think that is what helped by night time cortisol, but I think all of the pregnenolone is being shunted to cortisol. So I should stop the pregnenolone? Is it too much or do I just need to back off? I can't figure out why it is being converted to cortisol except I had a root canal about 2 weeks before testing. Could that have skewed my numbers?

4/15/22 7/22/12

9:00pm 2.1 (.4-1.0) 1.4

### Destiny — 2012-08-05T16:50:26+0300
